findings.summary: The payload says the phase has no test task and no test write target, but PHASE-XX.md explicitly declares both.
findings.remediation: Treat PHASE-XX.md lines 3 and 8 as authoritative: update the engineer work to create src/cache.test.kt and remove the false rationale from the delivery record.
findings.why_real_risk: This false scope interpretation already caused attempt 4 to omit required test work and can keep the fix loop exhausted even though the phase text is clear.
findings.repro: Open PHASE-XX.md line 3 and line 8, then compare them with engineer-payload.json lines 15-20; the payload asserts the opposite of the phase declaration.
tests_to_add: Add a phase-review fixture assertion that rejects payloads claiming a declared write file or task is absent when PHASE-XX.md contains it.

findings.summary: files_touched omits src/cache.test.kt even though PHASE-XX.md declares it in writes.
findings.remediation: Create src/cache.test.kt and add it to files_touched, or reject the phase attempt as incomplete until the declared write set is satisfied.
findings.why_real_risk: The phase contract requires both source and test artifacts; omitting the test file lets an incomplete cache implementation appear deliverable.
findings.repro: Compare PHASE-XX.md line 3 with engineer-payload.json lines 5-10; only src/cache.kt is reported touched, so the declared test artifact is absent.
tests_to_add: Add a validator test named rejects_payload_when_declared_write_file_is_missing_from_files_touched with PHASE writes containing src/cache.test.kt and payload touching only src/cache.kt.

findings.summary: The engineer explicitly skipped unit tests required by the phase task.
findings.remediation: Author src/cache.test.kt with tests covering insertion, eviction, and overflow for ISS-23ba7d51473d before accepting the phase.
findings.why_real_risk: The task specifically asks for test coverage of eviction and overflow; without it, regressions in capacity behavior can ship unnoticed.
findings.repro: Read PHASE-XX.md line 8, then inspect engineer-payload.json lines 12-20; the decision title is skip test authoring and the summary says tests were not authored.
tests_to_add: Add a unit test named put_then_get_returns_inserted_value that inserts one id and asserts get returns the stored value.
Add a unit test named put_beyond_capacity_evicts_expected_entry that fills the cache, inserts one extra entry, and asserts the eviction contract.
Add a unit test named overflow_does_not_grow_past_capacity that inserts more entries than capacity and asserts size or observable contents stay within the documented limit.

findings.summary: The payload reports creating src/cache.kt, but no src directory or cache.kt file exists in the reviewed workspace.
findings.remediation: Provide the actual src/cache.kt implementation or correct the payload so it does not claim a nonexistent deliverable.
findings.why_real_risk: A missing source file means the project cannot compile or satisfy the put/get API promised by the phase.
findings.repro: Run rg --files in the fixture; only TASK.md, PHASE-XX.md, and engineer-payload.json are present, while ls src fails because the directory does not exist.
tests_to_add: Add a phase-review test named rejects_payload_when_touched_file_is_absent that reports src/cache.kt in files_touched but leaves it missing on disk.

findings.summary: The reported implementation is only a simple HashMap-backed put/get cache, with no stated eviction or overflow behavior.
findings.remediation: Define and implement capacity behavior in src/cache.kt, including which entry is evicted and what happens when inserts exceed capacity, then cover it in src/cache.test.kt.
findings.why_real_risk: A cache without explicit capacity behavior fails the task's overflow requirement and can behave incorrectly once more entries are inserted than allowed.
findings.repro: Using the payload description, instantiate the cache, insert entries past the intended capacity, and call get on older and newer ids; there is no documented or tested result for eviction or overflow.
tests_to_add: Add a unit test named overflow_uses_documented_eviction_order that inserts capacity plus one entries and asserts the exact surviving ids.
Add a unit test named replacing_existing_id_does_not_trigger_extra_eviction that updates an existing id at capacity and asserts unrelated entries remain accessible.

findings.summary: A simple HashMap-backed cache with no overflow handling creates an availability risk under unbounded inserts.
findings.remediation: Enforce a fixed capacity or explicit overflow failure mode in src/cache.kt and test that repeated unique ids cannot grow storage without limit.
findings.why_real_risk: If callers can submit arbitrary ids, each unique id can allocate additional memory until the process slows down or terminates.
findings.repro: Loop over many unique ids and call put(id, value); with plain HashMap storage and no eviction or overflow path, retained entries grow with each unique insert.
tests_to_add: Add a property or stress-style unit test named many_unique_puts_respect_capacity that inserts far more unique ids than capacity and asserts retained entry count never exceeds capacity.

findings.summary: The delivery note presents a false reading of PHASE-XX.md as the converged interpretation after four attempts.
findings.remediation: Replace the rationale with an accurate status: tests and the declared test file are missing, so the phase is incomplete.
findings.why_real_risk: A misleading completion record wastes review cycles and can route the workflow toward a fixer despite the engineer payload being the source of the failure.
findings.repro: Compare the decision rationale with PHASE-XX.md line 8; the payload says no test task exists even though the phase has a dedicated test task.
tests_to_add: Add a review-fixture assertion named flags_false_scope_rationale that detects payload rationales contradicting explicit phase tasks.

not_fully_audited.reason: The engineer payload reports this file as created, but it is absent from the workspace, so implementation details could not be inspected.
not_fully_audited.recommended_followup: Provide the source file and rerun phase review against its actual contents.

not_fully_audited.reason: PHASE-XX.md declares this test file in writes, but the engineer did not create or report it.
not_fully_audited.recommended_followup: Create the required test file and rerun phase review.
